paper_title: Temperature and City Growth in European History

methods.description: This method describes the process of collecting and integrating data on European city sizes and temperature variations from historical records and climate proxies to study the impact of temperature changes on city growth.

steps.description: The main data set for this paper is a balanced panel of 2,120 European cities. Its two key components are data on annual temperature for Europe and data on city size in 1600, 1700, 1750, 1800, and 1850.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch historical temperature and city size data for European cities
outputs: balanced panel data set of 2,120 European cities

steps.description: Assign temperature data to each city based on the temperature grid cell in which the city is located.
steps.searchable_summary: map temperature data to cities based on grid location

steps.description: Combine the two data sets by calculating local mean temperature over the preceding 100 or 50 years for each time period.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate local mean temperature for specified time periods
outputs: integrated data set with mean temperatures

steps.description: Drop 71 cities from the original data set because temperature data are not available.
steps.searchable_summary: filter out cities with missing temperature data

steps.description: Use linear interpolation to fill missing values for time periods between nonzero values.
steps.searchable_summary: apply linear interpolation to fill missing data

steps.description: Assume cities below 1,000 inhabitants have 500 inhabitants when using the natural log of city size as the outcome variable.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust city size data for small cities
